headline: Second Nature Issues Allergy Alert for Keto Crunch Smart Mix Due to Undeclared Tree Nuts and Cherries

image.caption: Second Nature Issues Allergy Alert for Keto Crunch Smart Mix Due to Undeclared Tree Nuts and Cherries

description: Second Nature recalls Keto Crunch Smart Mix due to undeclared cashews, pistachios, and cherries that pose allergy risks. Distributed nationwide in 10-ounce pouches.
keywords: May contain undeclared Cashews, Pistachios, and Cherries

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Second Nature Brands has issued a recall for their Keto Crunch Smart Mix 10-ounce pouches because they may contain undeclared cashews, pistachios, and cherries, which are allergens not listed on the label.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The product involved in this allergy alert is the Keto Crunch Smart Mix by Second Nature Brands, packaged in a 10-ounce pouch with a UPC code of 077034013405. This product was distributed nationwide through retail stores and online orders.

The reason for the recall is the potential presence of undeclared allergens including cashews, pistachios, and cherries. These ingredients are tree nuts and fruit that can cause severe allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. The affected packages are specifically marked with a “Best if used by 2/12/2027” date on the backside of the pouch. Other packages with different best by dates are not affected.

As of the announcement on May 1, 2026, no illnesses or allergic reactions linked to this issue have been reported.

facts.reason: May contain undeclared Cashews, Pistachios, and Cherries

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check the packaging to identify if their product is affected by looking for the following details:

  • Product name: Keto Crunch Smart Mix
  • Package size: 10-ounce pouch
  • UPC code: 077034013405
  • "Best if used by" date printed on the backside of the pouch: 2/12/2027

Only products matching all these identifiers are affected. Packages with other best by dates are not subject to this recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have the affected Keto Crunch Smart Mix pouch, do not eat the product. Contact Second Nature Brands for instructions on product return or disposal. The company can be reached by phone at (800) 651-7263 or by email at recall@secondnaturebrandsus.com.

Individuals with allergies who have consumed this product and are experiencing any symptoms should seek immediate medical attention.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Undeclared allergens pose serious health risks to people with allergies or severe sensitivities. Consuming products containing cashews, pistachios, or cherries without this information on the label can lead to severe or life-threatening allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or received the Keto Crunch Smart Mix 10-ounce pouch by Second Nature with a "Best if used by 2/12/2027" date and UPC 077034013405 should check their product. This is especially important for individuals allergic to cashews, pistachios, or cherries.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Food allergies to tree nuts such as cashews and pistachios can trigger serious reactions, sometimes life-threatening. Cherries can also cause allergic responses in sensitive individuals. Accurate labeling is critical to help consumers avoid allergens. This recall highlights the importance of checking food labels carefully, especially for products marketed as specialty diets like keto mixes.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (800) 651-7263 | recall@secondnaturebrandsus.com

faq.answer: The affected product is a 10-ounce pouch with UPC 077034013405 and a "Best if used by" date of 2/12/2027 printed on the back.

faq.answer: No allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported in connection with this product issue as of the recall date.

headline: a2 Milk Company Recalls a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 Months USA Label for Possible Cereulide Toxin Contamination

image.caption: a2 Milk Company Recalls a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 Months USA Label for Possible Cereulide Toxin Contamination

description: The a2 Milk Company recalls specific batches of a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula USA label due to cereulide toxin presence, posing potential health risks for infants.
keywords: a2 Platinum Premium Infant Formula 0-12 months USA label
Presence of cereulide toxin produced by some strains of the bacterium Bacillus cereus

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The a2 Milk Company has voluntarily recalled three batches of its a2 Platinum Premium infant formula labeled for the USA market due to contamination with cereulide, a heat-stable toxin from Bacillus cereus, which may cause gastrointestinal illness especially in infants.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
The a2 Milk Company (a2MC) announced a voluntary recall on May 2, 2026, for three specific batches of the a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula designed for infants aged 0-12 months. This milk-based powdered formula contains iron and was distributed only in the United States via a2MC’s own website, Amazon, and Meijer stores as part of the Operation Fly Formula initiative. Importation rights expired on December 31, 2025, and the product was discontinued and removed from sale before the recall.

The recall was initiated after additional testing revealed the presence of cereulide, a toxin produced by some Bacillus cereus strains, likely introduced through an ingredient in the formula. The three affected batches total 63,078 units, with approximately 16,428 units sold to consumers. The affected batches should not be used despite no confirmed illnesses.

Consumers who purchased the formula are advised to check the batch number and expiration date on the bottom of the tins to identify affected products and stop using them immediately.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To determine if your formula is affected, locate the batch number and expiration date labeled "Use By" on the bottom of the 31.7 oz tins. The affected batches are:

  • Batch number 2210269454 with Use By date 7/15/2026
  • Batch number 2210324609 with Use By date 1/21/2027
  • Batch number 2210321712 with Use By date 1/15/2027

Only tins matching these batch numbers and dates are subject to the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have any of the recalled batches, immediately discontinue use of the formula. Consumers should either dispose of the product properly or return it to the place of purchase for a refund. If your infant shows symptoms such as nausea or vomiting after consuming this formula, contact a healthcare provider promptly.

For questions or concerns, consumers can contact the a2 Milk Company at 1-844-422-6455, Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 5 PM Eastern Time, or visit their website at a2platinum.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Cereulide is a toxin produced by certain strains of Bacillus cereus bacteria. It is heat-stable, meaning it is not destroyed by heating, including during formula preparation with hot water. Exposure to cereulide-contaminated food can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, usually occurring within 30 minutes to six hours after ingestion and commonly resolving within 24 hours. Because infants have developing immune systems, they are more vulnerable to complications such as dehydration, which can require medical attention.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Parents and caregivers who have purchased the a2 Platinum Premium USA label infant formula 0-12 months from a2MC’s website, Amazon, or Meijer stores should check if their product is part of the recall. The recall specifically applies to three batches of the product as identified by batch numbers and “Use By” dates located on the bottom of each tin.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Infant formula contamination poses particular health risks due to infants’ sensitive and developing immune systems. Bacillus cereus can produce cereulide, a toxin that survives heat treatment and can cause rapid-onset gastrointestinal illness. Prompt recognition of symptoms and product withdrawal can prevent dehydration and more serious complications in infants.

headline: B. Braun Medical Inc. Voluntarily Recalls Lactated Ringer’s Injection 1L (E7500) Due to Particulate Matter in Solution

image.caption: B. Braun Medical Inc. Voluntarily Recalls Lactated Ringer’s Injection 1L (E7500) Due to Particulate Matter in Solution

description: B. Braun Medical Inc. recalls Lactated Ringer’s Injection 1L (E7500) nationwide due to particulate matter contamination posing serious health risks to patients.
keywords: Products were found to contain particulate matter in solution

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: B. Braun Medical Inc. is voluntarily recalling two lots of Lactated Ringer’s Injection 1L (product code E7500) distributed nationwide after discovering particulate matter in the solution, which may cause serious harm if infused.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
B. Braun Medical Inc. announced a voluntary nationwide recall on April 28, 2026, of two lots of Lactated Ringer’s Injection, 1L, identified by product code E7500 and NDC 0264-7750-07. This product is used in hospitals and healthcare facilities as a source of electrolytes and water for hydration in both adult and pediatric patients.

The recall was initiated after complaints identified the presence of particulate matter—specifically cellulose, stearates, and trace inorganic materials such as copper—in the solution. Distribution was to the U.S. nationwide market, including distributors and healthcare providers, with expiration dates up to May 31, 2027.

To date, B. Braun has not received reports of adverse events related to this recall. The company is notifying distributors by mail and arranging returns of all recalled products to prevent use.

E75000264-7750-07LAC RNG INJ 1L E3J4S84313JAN2025 – 15MAY202531MAY2027US

facts.reason: Products were found to contain particulate matter in solution

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Check the product label for the following identifying details to determine if you have a recalled product:

  • Product Catalog Number: E7500
  • NDC Number: 0264-7750-07
  • Product Description: Lactated Ringer’s Injection 1L (label may show "LAC RNG INJ 1L E3")
  • Lot Numbers: J4P756 or J4S843
  • Expiration Date: May 31, 2027
  • Distributed in the U.S.

The product is supplied sterile in plastic containers, commonly packaged 12 per case. If any of these label details match, the product is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you are a healthcare provider or distributor with these recalled lots, immediately stop using the product and follow the instructions provided by B. Braun to return the units using the Urgent Pharmaceutical Recall Acknowledgement form.

If you received the product and have questions or need assistance, contact B. Braun’s Recalls Department at 844-903-6417 or email recalls@bbraunusa.com during business hours (Monday–Friday, 8:00 am – 5:00 pm EST).

If you are a patient who may have been exposed or has experienced any adverse symptoms, contact your physician or healthcare provider promptly.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The presence of particulate matter in an intravenous solution is concerning because particles entering the bloodstream can block blood vessels, potentially causing serious complications such as pulmonary embolism, tissue damage from blocked blood flow, vein inflammation, and systemic immune reactions. Such events can require additional medical treatment and may result in permanent injury or death.

Because the particles found include cellulose and stearates, substances not intended for infusion, their presence increases these risks. The concern is especially important since this solution is administered directly into the circulatory system for hydration and electrolyte replenishment.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects hospitals, healthcare facilities, distributors, and any consumers who have received or are using Lactated Ringer’s Injection, 1L with product code E7500 or NDC 0264-7750-07 from the two recalled lots. The recalled lot numbers are J4P756 and J4S843 with distribution ranges between November 15, 2024 and May 15, 2025.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Intravenous fluids like Lactated Ringer’s Injection are critical for hydration and electrolyte balance but must be free of contaminants to ensure patient safety. Particulate matter in such solutions poses significant risk as particles can travel through blood vessels, cause blockages, inflammation, and trigger immune system reactions. Ensuring intravenous medications are sterile, clear, and particulate-free is a fundamental safety standard in healthcare.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: B. Braun’s Recalls Department | 844-903-6417 | recalls@bbraunusa.com

faq.question: What is the reason for the B. Braun Lactated Ringer’s Injection recall?
faq.answer: The product was found to contain particulate matter in the solution, including cellulose, stearates, and trace inorganic materials, which poses a risk when infused into patients.

faq.question: Which specific lots of Lactated Ringer’s Injection are recalled?
faq.answer: Two lots are recalled: J4P756 with a distribution range from November 15, 2024 to March 6, 2025, and J4S843 distributed between January 13, 2025 and May 15, 2025.

faq.question: What should healthcare facilities do if they have the recalled product?
faq.answer: Facilities should immediately stop using the recalled lots and arrange for their return using the Urgent Pharmaceutical Recall Acknowledgement form provided by B. Braun.

faq.question: Are there any reported adverse events associated with this recall?
faq.answer: As of the recall announcement date, B. Braun has not received any reports of adverse events related to the recalled product.

faq.question: Who can I contact for questions about the recall?
faq.answer: You can contact B. Braun’s Recalls Department at 844-903-6417 or via email at recalls@bbraunusa.com Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm EST.

headline: Ghirardelli Recalls Select Powdered Beverage Mixes for Potential Salmonella Contamination

image.caption: Ghirardelli Recalls Select Powdered Beverage Mixes for Potential Salmonella Contamination

description: Ghirardelli is recalling certain powdered beverage mixes due to potential Salmonella contamination linked to ingredient milk powder, with no reported illnesses so far.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Ghirardelli Chocolate Company is voluntarily recalling specific powdered beverage mixes because they might be contaminated with Salmonella bacteria, which poses a health risk especially for vulnerable populations.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ghirardelli Chocolate Company of San Leandro, California, announced a voluntary recall of certain powdered beverage mixes. The recall comes after a related recall by California Dairies, Inc. involving milk powder suspected of possible Salmonella contamination. This milk powder was used as an ingredient in some Ghirardelli powdered beverage mixes.

The affected products include large package sizes commonly intended for food service and institutional customers, although some may have been sold to consumers via online platforms. As of the announcement date, no illnesses linked to these products have been reported.

The recall specifically identifies multiple lot numbers and best-if-used-by dates across various beverage mix types such as Chocolate Flavored Frappe, Classic White Frappe, Premium Hot Cocoa, and others. Consumers should refer to the detailed lot numbers and dates provided in the FDA and company announcements to verify if their product is impacted.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your powdered beverage mix is affected, compare the product name, lot number, and 'Best if Used by' date on the package label against the recall list provided by Ghirardelli and the FDA. The recall covers several products such as 30lb Chocolate Flavored Frappe (e.g., lot numbers S195260A03 with best by 5/31/2027), 6/3lb Vanilla Frappe Mix (e.g., lot S495260 with best by 3/31/2027), and multiple others.

Because the full list is extensive, consumers should consult the official FDA recall announcement or the product’s packaging for exact lot numbers and expiration dates to confirm if their product is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have a recalled powdered beverage mix, do not consume the product. You may contact Ghirardelli Chocolate Company at 1-844-776-0419 for more information or guidance; their consumer hotline operates 24/7.

Properly dispose of or return the product following instructions provided by the company or place of purchase. If you experience symptoms consistent with Salmonella infection after consuming these products, consult a healthcare professional promptly.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Salmonella is a bacteria that can cause foodborne illness, leading to symptoms such as fever, diarrhea (sometimes bloody), nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The infection poses higher risks to young children, elderly adults, people with weakened immune systems, and frail individuals. While healthy adults may recover without complications, Salmonella infections can, in rare cases, spread beyond the intestines causing serious illnesses such as bloodstream infections and infections of arteries, heart, and joints.

Because the recalled products have the potential to be contaminated with Salmonella, consuming them could lead to these health risks, especially in vulnerable populations.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
Consumers who have purchased any of the specific powdered beverage mixes listed in the recall should check their products immediately. This includes food service providers, institutional buyers, and individuals who may have bought these mixes through e-commerce platforms.

Those with large format packages or any products matching the recalled lot numbers are advised to verify if their product is included in the recall.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Salmonella contamination in food products is a serious safety concern because of the organism’s potential to cause illness. Powdered beverage mixes, like other dry food ingredients, can become contaminated during manufacturing if contaminated raw materials are used.

Maintaining strict quality controls in sourcing and processing ingredients helps reduce contamination risk. Consumers should handle powdered mixes according to package instructions, maintain clean preparation areas, and be aware of recalls to avoid consuming potentially unsafe products.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Ghirardelli Chocolate Company | 1-844-776-0419

faq.answer: The recall covers several powdered beverage mixes in large packaging formats, including Chocolate Flavored Frappe, Classic White Frappe, Premium Hot Cocoa, Chocolate & Cocoa Sweet Ground Powder, Vanilla Frappe Mix, White Chocolate Flavored Sweet Ground Powder, and others. Specific lot numbers and best-if-used-by dates are listed in the FDA and company announcements for precise identification.

faq.question: Why are these Ghirardelli powdered beverage mixes being recalled?
faq.answer: They are being recalled due to a potential contamination with Salmonella bacteria linked to an ingredient milk powder supplied by California Dairies, Inc., which was used in these products.

faq.question: Have any illnesses been reported related to this recall?
faq.answer: As of the recall announcement date, no illnesses linked to the recalled powdered beverage mixes have been reported.

headline: Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix Recalled Due to Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Cashews Allergens

image.caption: Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix Recalled Due to Undeclared Milk, Soy, and Cashews Allergens

description: We R Nuts recalls Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix due to undeclared milk, soy, and cashews allergens that may cause allergic reactions.
keywords: May contain undeclared milk, soy, and cashews

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: We R Nuts is recalling 254 containers of Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix because the product may contain undeclared milk, soy, and cashews, posing an allergy risk to sensitive individuals.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
We R Nuts, LLC of Port Washington, New York, is recalling 254 units of Uncle Giuseppe’s branded Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix in 11oz containers. The recall was initiated after an inspection revealed that the ingredient label did not declare milk, soy, and cashews, which are allergens. This omission means the product could cause allergic reactions in sensitive consumers.

The affected Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix, which contains chocolate-covered nuts and raisins, was sold in retail stores under the Uncle Giuseppe’s brand located in New York and New Jersey. The packaging consists of clear rectangular tamper-resistant plastic containers with sell-by dates between September 4, 2026, and November 6, 2026. The label also indicates "Distributed by We R Nuts" at the bottom.

To date, no illnesses or allergic reactions have been reported in connection with this recall. The company initiated the recall promptly after learning about the label discrepancy during a facility inspection.

facts.reason: May contain undeclared milk, soy, and cashews

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Check for Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix packaged in clear rectangular tamper-resistant 11oz plastic containers. The product labels include a sell-by date printed at the top left corner ranging from 9/4/2026 to 11/6/2026. At the bottom of the label, look for the statement "Distributed by We R Nuts." If your product matches these details, it is subject to the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have any allergies to milk, soy, or cashews and have purchased the recalled Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix, do not consume it. You should discard the product immediately and return it to the store where you bought it for a full refund. If you need more information or have questions about the recall, contact We R Nuts at (516) 705-0800, Monday through Friday, from 8:00 am to 3:30 pm Eastern Standard Time.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Milk, soy, and cashews are common allergens that can cause serious or life-threatening reactions in sensitive individuals. People who are allergic or severely sensitive to these ingredients may experience symptoms ranging from mild irritation to anaphylaxis, a severe allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Because these allergens were not declared on the product label, consumers may unknowingly consume them, increasing the risk of severe allergic responses.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who have allergies or severe sensitivities to milk, soy, or cashews and who purchased Uncle Giuseppe’s Milk Chocolate Bridge Mix in 11oz containers with sell-by dates between 9/4/2026 and 11/6/2026 should carefully check their products. This recall applies to items purchased from Uncle Giuseppe’s retail stores in New York and New Jersey.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Food allergens such as milk, soy, and tree nuts like cashews are among the most common causes of food allergy reactions. Individuals with allergies must avoid even trace amounts of these substances as reactions can rapidly escalate. Accurate labeling is essential to protect allergic consumers. When allergen information is missing or incorrect, it increases the risk of accidental exposure and serious health consequences.

headline: Recall Alert: Bette’s Bake Sale Bonbon Collection May Contain Undeclared Walnuts

image.caption: Recall Alert: Bette’s Bake Sale Bonbon Collection May Contain Undeclared Walnuts

description: French Broad Chocolates recalls Bette’s Bake Sale Bonbon Collection due to undeclared walnuts posing a risk to people with walnut allergies, distributed April 14-20.
keywords: Bette's Bake Sale Bonbon Collection in 6pc (2.5oz), 12pc (5oz), and 24pc (10oz) boxes

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: French Broad Chocolates is recalling Bette’s Bake Sale Bonbon Collection in 6, 12, and 24-piece boxes because they may contain undeclared walnuts, which could cause serious allergic reactions in people with walnut allergies.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
French Broad Chocolates PBC announced a recall of Bette’s Bake Sale Bonbon Collection boxes in 6-piece (2.5oz), 12-piece (5oz), and 24-piece (10oz) sizes. The recall involves products from batch numbers 260414 and 260417, with best-by dates in late June 2026. These were sold between April 14 and April 20, 2026, through French Broad Chocolates retail stores in Asheville, North Carolina, and online.

The recall is due to an undeclared tree nut allergen—walnuts—in the bonbons. Specifically, a labeling error on the tasting notes insert incorrectly switches the Walnut Fudge bonbon, which contains walnuts, with the Peach Cobbler bonbon. This mislabeling may cause consumers who rely on these notes to unknowingly eat nuts, risking allergic reactions.

No illnesses related to this issue have been reported to date. The recall is conducted voluntarily with FDA knowledge.

headline: Recall of Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix 9 oz. for Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens

image.caption: Recall of Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix 9 oz. for Undeclared Wheat and Soy Allergens

description: Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. recalls Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix 9 oz. due to undeclared wheat and soy allergens posing risk to allergy sufferers.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. is recalling a single lot of Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix 9 oz. because it may contain undeclared wheat and soy, allergens not listed on the packaging that pose a risk to consumers with allergies or severe sensitivities.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Ferris Coffee & Nut Co., based in Grand Rapids, Michigan, announced a recall on April 23, 2026, for a single lot of Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix 9 oz. The product was found to contain undeclared wheat and soy allergens due to a packaging error.

The affected product was distributed at Meijer retail stores in Michigan, Indiana, Ohio, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Kentucky. The issue arose after chocolate-covered pretzel balls containing wheat and soy were mistakenly packed instead of the listed bourbon-flavored caramels.

The lot can be identified by lot number 6069-1 and an expiration date of 12/10/26, both located on the back of the 9-ounce black packaging. To date, no illnesses connected to this recall have been reported.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Look for Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix in a black 9-ounce bag. Check the back of the package for lot number 6069-1 and an expiration date of 12/10/26. Both identifiers are required to confirm if the product is part of the recall.

If your product does not match both these details, it is not included in this recall. For further verification, compare details on the product packaging with the FDA notice or the company announcement linked on the FDA website.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have the recalled product, do not consume it. You should either discard it safely or return it to the store where you purchased it for a full refund.

If you have questions or want to confirm recall details, contact Ferris Coffee & Nut Co. consumer services at 1-877-363-4537 available from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM Eastern Daylight Time.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Wheat and soy are common allergens that can cause serious or life-threatening reactions in sensitive individuals. Consuming these undeclared allergens unknowingly could result in allergic responses ranging from mild symptoms to severe reactions such as anaphylaxis. Because the allergens were not listed on the product label, consumers relying on packaging information may be exposed to these risks.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who have purchased the 9-ounce Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix with lot # 6069-1 and expiration date 12/10/26 should check their packages. Especially those with allergies or severe sensitivities to wheat or soy need to verify if they have this specific product.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Food allergens like wheat and soy can trigger severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention. It is critical for people with food allergies to avoid consuming products with undeclared allergens. Manufacturers must accurately disclose allergen information on labels, as errors can pose serious health risks.

This recall highlights the importance of careful production and packaging controls in food manufacturing to prevent allergen cross-contact and protect sensitive consumers.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-877-363-4537

faq.answer: The recall involves undeclared wheat and soy allergens found in a specific lot of Frederik’s by Meijer Vanilla Bourbon Trail Mix.

faq.answer: The recalled product was distributed through Meijer retail stores in Michigan, Indiana, Ohio, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Kentucky.

faq.answer: Check the back of the 9-ounce black bag for lot number 6069-1 and expiration date 12/10/26. Both are needed to identify the recalled product.

headline: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions Recalls Angio Pack Kits with Medline Namic 10CC Syringe Because of Disconnection Risk

image.caption: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions Recalls Angio Pack Kits with Medline Namic 10CC Syringe Because of Disconnection Risk

description: Aligned Medical Solutions recalls Angio Pack kits containing Medline Namic 10CC syringes due to risk of adapter unwinding causing disconnection and potential serious.
keywords: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions

Risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which may result in a loose connection and/or full disconnection between the syringes and manifold, potentially causing biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Aligned Medical Solutions is recalling two Angio Pack convenience kits (AMS6908E and AMS6908F) that include the Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter 10CC Syringe. The adapter may unwind during use, risking syringe disconnection, which could cause biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 2, 2026, Aligned Medical Solutions of Billings, Montana, announced a nationwide recall of two convenience kits, AMS6908E Angio Pack and AMS6908F Angio Pack, due to a safety issue with an included Medline Namic Angiographic (RA) 10CC Syringe. These kits, distributed across the United States, were manufactured from October 18, 2024, through November 13, 2025, and distributed from October 28, 2024, through November 28, 2025.

The reason for the recall is a risk that the syringe rotating adapter may unwind during use. This issue can create a loose connection or full disconnection between the syringe and manifold, potentially leading to biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or the introduction of air into the bloodstream causing air embolism—a serious condition that can cause injury or death.

Medline reported three cases of inadvertent air injection and one biohazard exposure with these syringes, but no deaths have been reported. Aligned Medical Solutions is providing labels to identify recalled packs and offers replacement syringes upon request.

facts.company: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions
facts.reason: Risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which may result in a loose connection and/or full disconnection between the syringes and manifold, potentially causing biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Identify recalled products by examining the pack label inside the sterile barrier of the convenience kit. The affected kits are labeled as AMS6908E Angio Pack with Product Code B098AMS6908E0 and DI Number 214875, and AMS6908F Angio Pack with Product Code B098AMS6908F0 and DI Numbers 215436, 225001, 226706, and 234088. These kits include the Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter 10CC Syringe, part number 80085007. Facility staff should compare this information with their stock labels to verify if the kit is affected.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Facilities should immediately review and quarantine all kits identified as recalled. Do not use any Medline 10CC syringes included in these packs. Remove and destroy any recalled syringes according to your institution's medical waste policies to prevent inadvertent use. Contact Aligned Medical Solutions Quality Department at 406-259-6387 for labeling instructions and to request replacement syringes if needed. Distributors will be contacted directly for label arrangements. For further questions, contact the company at 1-800-360-9428 or email fieldcorrectiveaction@alignedmedicalsolutions.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The syringe rotating adapter unwinding means that during use, the syringe could detach unexpectedly. This detachment might allow air or contaminants into a patient’s bloodstream, potentially leading to serious health risks like air embolism (air bubbles blocking blood vessels), infections, or blood loss. Air embolism can cause serious injury or death, so this risk is significant in medical settings where these syringes are used.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Healthcare providers, hospitals, clinics, and other facilities that have received AMS6908E or AMS6908F Angio Pack kits containing the Medline Namic Angiographic 10CC Syringe should check their inventory immediately. Any patient who has recently undergone procedures using these syringes may also want to consult their healthcare professional.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Medical syringes used in angiographic procedures must maintain secure and sterile connections to avoid patient exposure to contaminants and air. Any disconnection or looseness during such procedures can introduce risks like infection, blood loss, or air entering the bloodstream. Air embolism is a rare but serious condition requiring immediate medical attention and prevention is critical. Proper identification and removal of potentially faulty devices help uphold patient safety.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-360-9428 | fieldcorrectiveaction@alignedmedicalsolutions.com

faq.answer: The recall involves two convenience kits: AMS6908E Angio Pack with Product Code B098AMS6908E0, DI Number 214875, and AMS6908F Angio Pack with Product Code B098AMS6908F0, DI Numbers 215436, 225001, 226706, and 234088.

faq.question: What is the main safety issue with the recalled syringes?
faq.answer: The Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter on the 10CC syringe may unwind during use, causing a loose or fully disconnected connection that can lead to biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

faq.question: What should I do if I have one of these recalled kits?
faq.answer: You should immediately quarantine the affected kits, remove and destroy any Medline 10CC syringes from use, and contact Aligned Medical Solutions for replacement syringes and instructions. Do not use the recalled syringes on patients.

headline: Revival Animal Health Recalls Canine Milk Replacers Due to Variable and Elevated Vitamin D

image.caption: Revival Animal Health Recalls Canine Milk Replacers Due to Variable and Elevated Vitamin D

description: Revival Animal Health recalls Breeder’s Edge and Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacers due to unsafe variable Vitamin D levels, including elevated amounts, which may.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Revival Animal Health is voluntarily recalling Breeder’s Edge Foster Care and Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacers because some products contain variable levels of Vitamin D, including elevated amounts that can pose health risks to dogs.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Revival Animal Health of Orange City, Iowa, announced a voluntary recall of its Breeder’s Edge Foster Care and Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacers. The recall was prompted by test results showing variable Vitamin D levels in some products, with some batches containing elevated amounts beyond recommended levels. These products are intended as milk replacers specifically formulated for puppies.

The affected products were distributed nationwide through online channels and retail pet stores. They come in multiple package sizes including 12 oz jars and stand-up resealable bags. A wide range of "best by" dates across various UPCs are involved. Revival Animal Health reported two complaints of rickets potentially related to this issue. The recall was conducted with the knowledge of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

Consumers are advised to stop feeding these products immediately. Contact details for the company are provided for refunds or further inquiries.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify affected products, compare the product name, net weight, UPC code, and "best by" dates on your package against the following details:

  • Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer: 1 lb (UPC 817160011087), 3.5 lb (UPC 817160011070), and 8 lb (UPC 817160011056) with various best by dates ranging from 6/19/2026 through 1/20/2028.
  • Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer: 12 oz jars (UPC 817160010189), 4.5 lb (UPC 817160010073), 18 lb (UPC 817160011025), and 20 lb (UPC 817160010172) with best by dates from 5/22/2026 through 2/5/2028.

Check the product packaging carefully for these identifiers. If you are uncertain, refer to the product label or the FDA announcement for complete details.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have any of the recalled products, stop feeding them to your dog immediately. Contact Revival Animal Health at 1-877-870-4059 (Monday through Friday, 8 am to 4 pm CDT) for instructions on how to return the product and receive a full refund.

If your dog has consumed the milk replacer and is showing any symptoms such as vomiting, loss of appetite, excessive thirst, frequent urination, drooling, or weight loss, consult your veterinarian promptly.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Vitamin D is essential for dogs but can be harmful in excessive amounts. Elevated Vitamin D intake may lead to symptoms such as vomiting, loss of appetite, increased thirst and urination, excessive drooling, and weight loss. Very high Vitamin D levels can cause serious conditions including renal (kidney) dysfunction, which can be life-threatening if untreated. This recall addresses the risk of both low and high Vitamin D levels, but the primary concern is the elevated levels in the affected canine milk replacers.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or currently has Breeder’s Edge Foster Care or Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacers should verify if their product is part of this recall. This includes dog owners, breeders, shelters, and rescue organizations nationwide who use these milk replacers for puppies.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Products like canine milk replacers are formulated to provide essential nutrition to puppies that cannot nurse adequately. Proper levels of vitamins, including Vitamin D, are critical for healthy development. Too little Vitamin D can cause bone disorders like rickets, while too much can lead to toxicity affecting the kidneys and other organs. This recall underscores the importance of consistent formulation and quality control in pet foods to prevent nutritional imbalances that can harm animals.

content.text: Consumer support: 1-877-870-4059

headline: Loard’s Ice Cream Recall Due to Undeclared Allergens and Food Color Additives

image.caption: Loard’s Ice Cream Recall Due to Undeclared Allergens and Food Color Additives

description: Loard’s Ice Cream is recalled for missing allergen labels including milk, eggs, nuts, peanuts, soy, wheat, sulfites, and certain food color additives in Northern.
keywords: Silver Moon LP dba Loard’s Ice Cream
Food & Beverages Ice Cream/Frozen Dairy Allergens
Undeclared Milk, Eggs, Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Soy, Sulfites, and Colors

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Silver Moon LP dba Loard’s Ice Cream is voluntarily recalling all retail-sized ice cream products sold at Loard’s parlors in Northern California because the packaging lacks proper ingredient labeling, leading to undeclared allergens, sulfites, and food colors that pose serious risks to sensitive consumers.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Silver Moon LP, doing business as Loard’s Ice Cream, based in San Leandro, California, issued a voluntary recall of all retail-sized ice cream products sold in Loard’s Ice Cream parlors across Northern California. The recall involves 32oz blue paper cups and 56oz plastic cups labeled under the Loard’s brand.

The recall was initiated after an FDA inspection revealed these products were distributed without the required ingredient statements and allergen declarations. Because of this, multiple allergens such as milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, and wheat, along with sulfites and certain food color additives, are undeclared on the packaging.

No illnesses have been reported to date related to this recall. Customers who purchased these products are encouraged to return them to the place of purchase for a refund or replacement with correctly labeled packaging.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check the ice cream packaging size and label details. The recalled products are sold in 32oz blue paper cups and 56oz plastic cups under the Loard’s Ice Cream brand. Specific ice cream flavors involved include, but are not limited to, Loard’s Almond Joy, Banana, Black Raspberry, Brownie Nut Fudge, Caramel Cashew, Egg Nog, Lime Sherbet, Maple Walnut, Peanut Butter Fudge, Pistachio, Rocky Road, Sea Salt Caramel, Ube, and Vanilla Ice Cream.

The packaging lacks ingredient and allergen declarations that are normally required. Comparing packaging to the label examples or ingredient list provided in the company recall notice can help identify affected products.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have purchased any Loard’s Ice Cream retail-sized products from Loard’s Ice Cream parlors in Northern California, do not consume the product if you have allergies or sensitivities to the undeclared allergens or sulfites. Instead, return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund or exchange for a product with updated labeling.

If you have questions or need assistance, contact Silver Moon LP dba Loard’s Ice Cream directly at (415) 547-0520, Monday through Friday, 9AM to 3PM PST, or via email at hello@silvermoonfoods.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Allergens like milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, and wheat can cause severe or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. Sulfites may trigger asthma and other reactions in some people. Inadequate labeling prevents consumers from making safe choices, placing them at risk of accidental exposure to substances they must avoid for health reasons. Undeclared food colors may also affect sensitive individuals, including those with allergies or intolerances.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or consumed Loard’s Ice Cream products in retail-sized containers from Loard’s Ice Cream parlors in Northern California should check if their product is part of the recall. This is particularly important for people with allergies or sensitivities to milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, wheat, sulfites, or to certain food color additives.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Proper allergen labeling on food products is critical to protect consumers who have allergies or severe sensitivities. Accidental ingestion of allergens like milk, eggs, nuts, peanuts, soy, wheat, or exposure to sulfites can lead to reactions ranging from mild discomfort to severe, life-threatening anaphylaxis. Frozen dairy products are common sources of these allergens, so accurate labeling helps consumers avoid unsafe exposures and manage their health safely.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: (415) 547-0520 | hello@silvermoonfoods.com

faq.answer: Undeclared allergens include milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, and wheat. Some products also contain undeclared sulfites and food color additives.

faq.answer: They were sold in Loard’s Ice Cream parlors located in Northern California.

headline: Liquid Blenz Corp recalls Good Brain Tonic carbonated soft drink due to potential botulism contamination

image.caption: Liquid Blenz Corp recalls Good Brain Tonic carbonated soft drink due to potential botulism contamination

description: Liquid Blenz Corp recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz & 32 oz bottles nationwide due to possible botulism contamination, posing a serious health risk.
keywords: Food & Beverages Carbonated Soft Drinks Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Liquid Blenz Corp is recalling all 16 oz and 32 oz bottles of Good Brain Tonic because of a potential risk of botulism, a serious and potentially fatal foodborne illness.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Liquid Blenz Corp of Rockville Center, NY is voluntarily recalling all codes of its Good Brain Tonic, a carbonated soft drink available in 16 oz and 32 oz amber bottles with plastic caps. This recall was announced due to a potential botulism risk identified through testing by the Cornell Food Venture Center and the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets Food Inspectors.

The product was distributed widely across the United States through retail stores and online sales. No illnesses linked to this product have been reported to date. The company urges consumers to return any affected product for a full refund.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Consumers should look for Good Brain Tonic carbonated soft drink bottles in 16 oz or 32 oz sizes. The bottles are amber-colored with plastic caps. The 16 oz bottles have UPC code 860010984468, and the 32 oz bottles have UPC code 860010984475. Detailed product labels and codes can also be cross-checked against information available on the product packaging or from the FDA recall announcement.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have purchased Good Brain Tonic 16 oz or 32 oz bottles, do not consume them. Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. If you experience any symptoms consistent with botulism after consuming this product, seek immediate medical attention. For further questions, consumers can contact Liquid Blenz Corp at 1-516-608-8826.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Botulism is a severe form of food poisoning caused by toxins produced by the bacteria Clostridium botulinum. It can lead to symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, and difficulties with speaking or swallowing. In more serious cases, it may cause breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal swelling, and constipation. Prompt medical attention is critical if symptoms appear after consuming the recalled product.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Good Brain Tonic in either the 16 oz or 32 oz amber bottles with plastic caps should check their products carefully. This includes anyone who bought the drink at retail stores or through internet sales anywhere in the United States.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Carbonated soft drinks are generally low-risk for serious foodborne illness, but contamination with Clostridium botulinum is especially dangerous because the toxin it produces can cause life-threatening botulism. Proper manufacturing and storage are essential to prevent contamination. Awareness and prompt response to recalls like this help reduce risk and protect public health.

headline: Morovan 15ml Gel Nail Polish Remover Recalled for Containing Hazardous Prohibited Chemicals

image.caption: Morovan 15ml Gel Nail Polish Remover Recalled for Containing Hazardous Prohibited Chemicals

description: Morovan recalls 15ml Gel Nail Polish Remover due to methylene chloride and chloroform contamination; consumers should stop use and dispose safely.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Morovan has recalled its 15ml Gel Nail Polish Remover after testing detected methylene chloride and chloroform, both prohibited and hazardous substances not allowed in cosmetics. Consumers should stop using the product immediately and follow instructions for safe disposal and refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Morovan's recall involves the Gel Nail Polish Remover, sold in 15ml (0.5 fl. oz.) square turquoise bottles with white caps and green packaging boxes manufactured in China. The product is intended for fast, safe, and gentle removal of gel nail polish and builder gel. Approximately 4,000 units were sold primarily on Amazon.com from August 2025 through January 2026.

The recall was issued after the detection of methylene chloride and chloroform in the product. Methylene chloride is a known carcinogen in animal studies and is prohibited in all cosmetic products by FDA regulations. The presence of these chemicals makes the product unsafe for consumer use. No injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection with this recall to date.

Consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and take steps to ensure proper disposal and refund as outlined by Morovan's recall announcement dated April 9, 2026.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled product, look for the following details on the label and packaging:

  • Brand: Morovan
  • Product: Gel Nail Polish Remover
  • Size: 15ml (0.5 fl. oz.)
  • Bottle: Square turquoise bottle with a white cap
  • Box: Matching green packaging box
  • Manufactured in China with production date 02-07-2024 and expiration date 02-07-2027

If your product matches these details, it is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have the recalled Morovan Gel Nail Polish Remover, stop using it immediately. Clearly mark the bottle and outer packaging with the word “Recalled” in a visible spot.

Then, safely dispose of the entire product, including the bottle and any remaining contents, in your regular household waste following your local disposal regulations.

After disposal, take a photo showing the product with the “Recalled” label visible and email it to Morovan’s customer service at customer-service@morovan.com to verify your refund eligibility.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Methylene chloride and chloroform are hazardous chemicals not permitted in cosmetics. Methylene chloride has been linked to cancer in animal studies and may pose serious health risks to humans, especially with direct skin contact or inhalation. The presence of these substances in nail polish remover is a significant safety concern because consumers apply the product on their nails and are exposed through skin and fumes.

Using this recalled nail polish remover could increase the risk of harmful chemical exposure, so discontinuing use and careful disposal is important to protect your health.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects all consumers who purchased or received the Morovan Gel Nail Polish Remover 15ml bottles sold on Amazon.com between August 2025 and January 2026. If you have this product in your possession, you should check it promptly before further use.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Nail polish removers often contain chemicals that can be harmful if misused. Methylene chloride is banned in cosmetics because it can cause cancer and other health problems. This recall helps prevent exposure to such hazardous substances, reinforcing the importance of only using cosmetic products compliant with FDA safety standards.

Consumers should always review product ingredient labels and comply with recall instructions to reduce health risks.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: customer-service@morovan.com

faq.question: Why was the Morovan Gel Nail Polish Remover recalled?
faq.answer: The product was recalled because testing found methylene chloride and chloroform, two chemicals prohibited and considered hazardous in cosmetic products.

faq.question: What should I do if I have this nail polish remover?
faq.answer: Stop using it immediately, mark the product and box as "Recalled," dispose of it safely according to local regulations, then email a photo showing the disposed product to customer-service@morovan.com for refund verification.

faq.question: Were there any illnesses reported from this product?
faq.answer: To date, no injuries or illnesses have been reported in connection to this recalled product.

headline: Blueroot Health Expands Recall of Vital Nutrients Aller-C Dietary Supplements for Undeclared Egg, Hazelnut, and Soy
description: Blueroot Health recalls Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements due to undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy posing allergy risks to sensitive consumers.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Blueroot Health has expanded a voluntary recall for its Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements because some packages may contain undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy allergens, posing a risk to those allergic or sensitive to these ingredients.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 10, 2026, Blueroot Health announced an expansion of its voluntary recall for the Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements. The recall covers all products manufactured under lot number 25E04, including previously recalled lots 25E04-A and 25E04-B. The reason for the recall is the potential presence of undeclared egg, hazelnut, and soy allergens in these dietary supplements.

The Aller-C supplements are packaged in white plastic bottles containing either 100 or 200 capsules. The recalled lots are identified by lot numbers 25E04, 25E04-A, and 25E04-B with an expiration date of 05/27 printed on the side of the bottle. Distribution occurred nationwide through VitalNutrients.co and other online retailers from September 2025 through March 2026.

The company discovered the undeclared allergens during routine internal testing and promptly reported the issue to the FDA. As of the announcement, no illnesses have been reported related to the consumption of this product.

ProductCountUPCLot NumbersExpiration Date
Vital Nutrients Aller-C200693465524213, 69346500009025E04-B05/27
Vital Nutrients Aller-C100693465524114, 69346500008325E04, 25E04-A, 25E04-B05/27

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if you have the recalled product, examine the bottle for the following details:

  • Brand: Vital Nutrients Aller-C
  • Package size: bottles containing either 100 or 200 capsules
  • Lot numbers: 25E04, 25E04-A, or 25E04-B
  • Expiration date: 05/27 (printed on the side of the bottle)
  • UPC codes: 693465524114 and 693465000083 for 100-count bottles; 693465524213 and 693465000090 for 200-count bottles

Compare these details to your product packaging to determine if your supplement is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If you have the affected product, immediately stop using it. Consumers are advised to return the recalled product to Blueroot Health for a replacement. For questions or further assistance, contact Blueroot Health Customer Service at (888) 328-9992 Monday through Friday from 8:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. or email support@vitalnutrients.co.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Undeclared allergens in dietary supplements can cause serious and potentially life-threatening allergic reactions in individuals who have allergies or severe sensitivities to egg, hazelnut, or soy. These reactions may include symptoms such as difficulty breathing, swelling, or anaphylaxis, which require immediate medical attention. Because these allergens were not listed on the product label, consumers with allergies might unknowingly consume the product and be at risk.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who have purchased Vital Nutrients Aller-C dietary supplements from lot numbers 25E04, 25E04-A, or 25E04-B with an expiration date of 05/27 should check their product. This recall is particularly important for people allergic to or sensitive to egg, hazelnut, or soy, as they face increased health risks from consuming these supplements.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Allergic reactions to undeclared ingredients in dietary supplements can be severe, especially for allergens like egg, hazelnut, and soy. It is important that all allergens are clearly listed to prevent accidental exposure. Consumers with known allergies should always carefully read product labels and heed recall notices to avoid health risks. Dietary supplements can vary in ingredient sources, making vigilance necessary for allergy safety.

headline: Blaine Labs Voluntarily Recalls Revitaderm and Tridergel Wound Care Gels Due to Lysinibacillus fusiformis Contamination

image.caption: Blaine Labs Voluntarily Recalls Revitaderm and Tridergel Wound Care Gels Due to Lysinibacillus fusiformis Contamination

description: Blaine Labs recalls Revitaderm and Tridergel wound care gels due to contamination with Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental bacterium that can pose infection.
keywords: Antiseptic wound care gel bottles and tubes
Products were found to contain Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental organism

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Blaine Labs has voluntarily recalled specific lots of Revitaderm and Tridergel antiseptic wound care gels after detecting contamination with Lysinibacillus fusiformis. This environmental bacterium may pose infection risks, especially for people with compromised immune systems or open wounds.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 7, 2026, Blaine Labs, Inc. announced a nationwide voluntary recall of certain lots of its antiseptic wound care gels under the brand names Revitaderm and Tridergel. The recalled products include 1 oz. and 3 oz. bottles and tubes containing 0.1% Benzalkonium Chloride. The recall was initiated after microbial testing identified the presence of Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental, spore-forming bacterium.

The specific lots affected are BL3608 (expiring 07/01/28), BL3435 (expiring 11/06/26), and BL3525 (expiring 08/07/27), recognizable by ink color and placement on bottles or tubes. The products were distributed to consumers nationwide. To date, Blaine Labs has not received any reports of adverse health events related to this contamination.

The company is notifying healthcare providers and arranging returns of undispensed products for these lot numbers. Consumers who have these products should take precautions due to potential microbial contamination.

facts.product: Antiseptic wound care gel bottles and tubes
facts.reason: Products were found to contain Lysinibacillus fusiformis, an environmental organism

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Check the following details on your wound care gel product to determine if it is part of the recall:

  • Brand: Revitaderm or Tridergel
  • Product form: 1 oz. or 3 oz. bottles or tubes
  • Active ingredient: Benzalkonium Chloride 0.1%
  • Lot numbers and expiration dates:
    • BL3608, expiring 07/01/28 (Yellow ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle, shorter twist cap)
    • BL3435, expiring 11/06/26 (Black ink on bottom of 1 oz. bottle with long pointy cap, or imprinted on 3 oz. tube crimp)

For products not clearly marked or if you are uncertain, verify the lot number and expiration date by checking the product label or contacting Blaine Labs directly.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
Immediately stop using any Revitaderm or Tridergel wound care gel from the recalled lot numbers. Segregate these products from other medications or supplies to prevent accidental use. Contact Blaine Labs, Inc. to arrange for the return of any remaining products. The company can be reached Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 4:00 PM PST, by phone at (800) 307-8818 or by email at Contact@blainelabs.com.

If you have used the product and experience any unusual symptoms, skin irritation, or signs of infection around your wounds, consult a healthcare professional promptly. Adverse events or quality problems can also be reported to the FDA’s MedWatch program online or via mail/fax using forms available on the FDA website.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Lysinibacillus fusiformis is an environmental bacterium that can survive harsh conditions. While it is generally considered low risk for healthy individuals, it can be an opportunistic pathogen. This means it may cause infections or delays in wound healing, particularly in people with open wounds, weakened immune systems, or compromised skin barriers.

Individuals at higher risk include the elderly, diabetic patients, immunocompromised persons, and young children. In rare cases, infections may become serious and lead to complications such as infection of the heart lining (endocarditis) or central nervous system involvement. The recall is a precaution to minimize any risk of infection or irritation from contaminated wound care products.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who currently owns or uses Revitaderm or Tridergel antiseptic wound care gels from the specified recalled lots. Particularly, individuals treating wounds or those with compromised immune systems should carefully check their products. Healthcare providers and consumers who may have these specific lots in their possession or inventory should review their products immediately.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Wound care antiseptic gels help prevent infection and support healing of minor cuts and wounds. However, if these products become contaminated with bacteria, especially spore-forming types like Lysinibacillus fusiformis, they can potentially cause infections or delay healing. Maintaining sterile products and proper storage is critical for safety.

People with open wounds or compromised immune systems should be especially cautious with wound care products and consult healthcare providers if infections or adverse reactions are suspected. This recall highlights the importance of product quality control to minimize health risks.

headline: Nalpac Voluntarily Recalls DTF Sexual Chocolate Due to Hidden Prescription Ingredients Sildenafil and Tadalafil

image.caption: Nalpac Voluntarily Recalls DTF Sexual Chocolate Due to Hidden Prescription Ingredients Sildenafil and Tadalafil

description: Nalpac recalls DTF Sexual Chocolate nationwide for undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, which may pose health risks for consumers on certain prescription drugs.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Nalpac is voluntarily recalling DTF Sexual Chocolate because it contains undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients sildenafil and tadalafil. These substances are prescription drugs used to treat erectile dysfunction and are not listed on the product label, raising safety concerns especially for people on certain medications.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Nalpac is recalling DTF Sexual Chocolate, a food product sold as a chocolate candy. The recall is due to the presence of undeclared sildenafil and tadalafil, which are active pharmaceutical ingredients in the prescription drugs Viagra and Cialis, respectively. These drugs are intended for use only under medical supervision.

The recall was initiated after it was discovered that these ingredients were present in the product without disclosure on the label. Nalpac purchased and resold the product but is not the manufacturer.

The recalled chocolate was packaged in cases with 20 retail units each. It was available in adult retail stores and online through shopsexology.com and royalsins.com. Consumers can identify the product by the UPC code 757817783069 on the packaging. As of the recall, no adverse events have been reported to Nalpac.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Consumers should look for the product name "DTF Sexual Chocolate" and check the packaging for the UPC code 757817783069. The product label and packaging should match those pictured in the FDA announcement. If in doubt, compare the product label directly with the images or description in the FDA recall notice.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have DTF Sexual Chocolate, stop using and do not consume the product. Consumers and retailers should return the product to the place of purchase or discard it immediately. For additional questions, contact Nalpac at 248-541-1140 or email sales@nalpac.com Monday through Friday from 8:00 am to 4:30 pm Eastern Time.

If you have experienced any adverse health effects associated with this product, contact your healthcare provider. You can also report problems to the FDA's MedWatch program online or by mail or fax as directed in the FDA announcement.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Sildenafil and tadalafil can affect blood pressure and interact dangerously with some prescription medications, particularly nitrates used to treat heart conditions. If taken unknowingly, these ingredients may cause serious side effects including dangerously low blood pressure. Because these ingredients are not declared, consumers might unknowingly consume them, which is a significant safety concern.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or received DTF Sexual Chocolate should check their product. This is especially important for individuals who take prescription medications containing nitrates or have cardiovascular conditions, as these undeclared ingredients can interact with such medications.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Food products containing undeclared pharmaceutical ingredients pose health risks because consumers cannot make informed decisions or receive medical guidance. Sildenafil and tadalafil require medical oversight due to potential interactions and side effects. Consumer safety relies on accurate labeling and transparency about product contents, especially for products marketed as foods but containing drug ingredients.

content.text: Consumer support: 248-541-1140 | sales@nalpac.com

faq.answer: It was sold in adult retail stores and online through shopsexology.com and royalsins.com.

headline: Wawa Issues Recall for Certain 16-oz Beverage Pints Due to Possible Milk Allergen

image.caption: Wawa Issues Recall for Certain 16-oz Beverage Pints Due to Possible Milk Allergen

description: Wawa recalls select 16-oz iced teas, diet lemonade, and fruit punch for undeclared milk allergen risk in limited stores across five states. No illnesses reported.
keywords: Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade and Fruit Punch
Potential or Undeclared Allergen – Undeclared Milk

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Wawa has recalled specific 16-ounce pints of Wawa Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch sold in certain stores in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, and Virginia due to the possible presence of an undeclared milk allergen. This recall is a precautionary measure to protect individuals with milk allergies.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 3, 2026, Wawa announced a recall of select 16-ounce pints of its Wawa branded Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, and Fruit Punch. These products were sold in a limited number of Wawa stores located in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, and Virginia. The recall was initiated after a temporary equipment issue was identified and corrected, which could have caused the presence of an undeclared milk allergen in the affected beverages.

Recalled products were promptly removed from sale and disposed of by the impacted stores. Other states where Wawa operates are not affected by this recall, and no other Wawa beverage products are included. To date, there have been no reported illnesses linked to the consumption of these recalled items.

The affected products include four varieties packaged in 16 oz pint bottles with specific UPC codes and code dates. Stores impacted vary by product, totaling several hundred locations across the specified states. Consumers can verify affected stores and product images on Wawa's recall alert webpage.

The code date is printed on the top of the bottle. If your product matches one of these UPCs and code dates, and you purchased it from one of the specified stores, your product may be affected by this recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have purchased any of the recalled Wawa 16-ounce beverage pints listed above, do not consume them. Dispose of the product immediately to avoid risk of allergic reaction.

For questions or to request a refund in the form of a Wawa Gift Card, consumers can contact the Wawa 24 hour Customer Contact Center at 1-800-444-9292 or reach out via their official website.

Consumers may also visit Wawa’s official recall alert page online for additional details including impacted store locations and product images.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Milk is a common food allergen that can cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. When milk is not listed on a product's label but is present, people with milk allergies may unknowingly consume it and suffer severe health effects. This is why undeclared allergens on food and beverage products represent an important safety risk and must be addressed swiftly.

This recall aims to prevent accidental consumption of milk-containing products by those who need to avoid it. While no illnesses have been reported, anyone allergic to milk should avoid the recalled beverages entirely, as reactions could be severe.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
Consumers who have purchased 16-ounce pints of Wawa Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, or Fruit Punch from Wawa stores in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, or Virginia are urged to check their products. Specifically, people allergic to milk should be especially vigilant because the recalled products may contain milk not disclosed on the label.

If you are unsure whether your product is included in the recall, you can compare the product details outlined below or consult Wawa’s recall information online or on product packaging.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Food allergies, especially to milk, can lead to severe reactions including anaphylaxis, which can be life-threatening and requires immediate medical attention. Undeclared allergens on food and beverage products pose a critical public health concern because they prevent consumers from making informed choices.

Retailers and manufacturers follow strict labeling regulations to prevent undeclared allergens. When such incidents occur, recalls are necessary to protect allergic consumers and maintain public safety.

Milk is a common food allergen that can cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. When milk is not listed on a product's label but is present, people with milk allergies may unknowingly consume it and suffer severe health effects. This is why undeclared allergens on food and beverage products represent an important safety risk and must be addressed swiftly.

This recall aims to prevent accidental consumption of milk-containing products by those who need to avoid it. While no illnesses have been reported, anyone allergic to milk should avoid the recalled beverages entirely, as reactions could be severe.
editorialBrief.whoIsAffectedHtml:
Consumers who have purchased 16-ounce pints of Wawa Iced Tea Lemon, Iced Tea Diet Lemon, Diet Lemonade, or Fruit Punch from Wawa stores in Pennsylvania, Delaware, Maryland, New Jersey, or Virginia are urged to check their products. Specifically, people allergic to milk should be especially vigilant because the recalled products may contain milk not disclosed on the label.

If you are unsure whether your product is included in the recall, you can compare the product details outlined below or consult Wawa’s recall information online or on product packaging.
editorialBrief.howToIdentifyProductHtml:
Check the label on your 16 oz pint bottle for the following product details:

  • Wawa Iced Tea Lemon: UPC 726191018425 with code date MAY 15, 2026 (printed on top of the bottle), available in 123 stores across DE, MD, NJ, PA, and VA.
  • Wawa Iced Diet Tea Lemon: UPC 726191018548 with code date MAY 18, 2026, found in 8 stores in NJ and PA.
  • Wawa Diet Lemonade: UPC 726191055901 with code date May 18, 2026, sold in 12 stores in DE and NJ.
  • Wawa Fruit Punch: UPC 726191018432 with code date May 19, 2026, available in 53 stores in DE, MD, NJ, PA, and VA.

The code date is printed on the top of the bottle. If your product matches one of these UPCs and code dates, and you purchased it from one of the specified stores, your product may be affected by this recall.
editorialBrief.whatToDoHtml:
If you have purchased any of the recalled Wawa 16-ounce beverage pints listed above, do not consume them. Dispose of the product immediately to avoid risk of allergic reaction.

For questions or to request a refund in the form of a Wawa Gift Card, consumers can contact the Wawa 24 hour Customer Contact Center at 1-800-444-9292 or reach out via their official website.

Consumers may also visit Wawa’s official recall alert page online for additional details including impacted store locations and product images.
editorialBrief.healthAndSafetyContextHtml:
Food allergies, especially to milk, can lead to severe reactions including anaphylaxis, which can be life-threatening and requires immediate medical attention. Undeclared allergens on food and beverage products pose a critical public health concern because they prevent consumers from making informed choices.

Retailers and manufacturers follow strict labeling regulations to prevent undeclared allergens. When such incidents occur, recalls are necessary to protect allergic consumers and maintain public safety.
editorialBrief.sourceTransparencyHtml:

The FDA shares the company's recall announcements as a public service and does not endorse the product or company.

For full details, consumers should reference the FDA recall page and Wawa’s official communications linked in the announcement.

headline: RAW FARM Recalls Select Cheddar Cheese Products Due to Possible E. coli Contamination
description: RAW FARM is voluntarily recalling select shredded and block cheddar cheese for potential E. coli contamination. Check labels carefully and do not consume affected.
keywords: Raw milk cheddar cheese, shredded and block

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: RAW FARM announced a voluntary recall of certain shredded and block cheddar cheese products due to potential contamination with E. coli bacteria. No pathogens have been found in tested samples, but the recall is issued as a precaution to protect public health.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
On April 2, 2026, RAW FARM voluntarily recalled select batches of their raw milk cheddar cheese products, including both shredded and block forms. This recall was initiated following an FDA advisory related to a potential E. coli contamination linked epidemiologically to these products. The recall includes multiple specific batches identified by their item codes, package sizes, barcodes, batch numbers, and expiration dates, including RAW CHEDDAR Original, RAW CHEDDAR Simply Shredded, and RAW CHEDDAR Jalapeno varieties.

No pathogens, including E. coli, have been detected in samples collected by the FDA or state health officials from RAW FARM cheese products. RAW FARM disputes the epidemiological links but is issuing this voluntary recall in the interest of public safety. The recall applies only to the RAW FARM-brand cheddar cheese products listed and not to other products or brands.

Consumers are advised not to consume the affected batches and to return them to the point of purchase for a full refund. For additional details regarding the FDA advisory and ongoing outbreak investigation, consumers can consult the FDA website.
