content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your product is part of this recall, compare the following label details on your RAW FARM cheddar cheese package to the recalled batches:

  • Item Description (e.g., RAW CHEDDAR Original, RAW CHEDDAR Simply Shredded, RAW CHEDDAR Jalapeno)
  • Package Type (Block or Bag)
  • Package Size in ounces (8, 16, 80 oz.)
  • Barcode number
  • Batch number
  • Expiration date

All these details should be on the product packaging. The recall covers specific batches with those identifiers and any batches produced prior to those dates. If you are unsure, check the official FDA recall announcement or the product label carefully before consuming.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: If your RAW FARM cheddar cheese matches the recalled batches, do not consume it. Instead, return the product to the store where it was purchased to request a full refund. Do not attempt to consume or distribute the product. If you have symptoms of foodborne illness after consuming any cheese, contact a healthcare professional promptly. For other questions or concerns, consumers can contact RAW FARM directly via their official website contact link provided in the announcement.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: E. coli is a type of bacteria that can cause foodborne illness, sometimes leading to serious health issues like severe stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting. While not all strains of E. coli are harmful, some can cause significant gastrointestinal upset and complications, especially in young children, the elderly, or people with weakened immune systems. Potential contamination of dairy products, like raw milk cheddar cheese, poses a risk because the bacteria can multiply if the product is consumed. This recall aims to prevent any potential cases of illness by removing possibly contaminated products from the market, despite no confirmed detections of E. coli in tested cheese samples.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or currently possesses RAW FARM-brand raw milk cheddar cheese products matching the recalled batches should carefully check their products. This includes consumers, retailers, and distributors who may have received the affected shredded or block cheddar cheese varieties in the specified package sizes and batch identifiers listed in the recall announcement.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Raw milk products like raw milk cheddar cheese carry increased risk of contamination by harmful bacteria, including E. coli, because they are not pasteurized to kill pathogens. E. coli contamination can result in foodborne illness outbreaks. Recalls such as this are an important public health measure to minimize exposure to potentially contaminated foods and prevent illness. Food safety experts generally recommend consuming pasteurized dairy products to reduce the risk of bacterial infections.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: https://rawfarmusa.com/contact

faq.question: Has E. coli been confirmed in RAW FARM cheddar cheese products?
faq.answer: No E. coli or other pathogens have been detected in the RAW FARM cheddar cheese samples tested by the FDA or state health departments. The recall is a precautionary measure based on epidemiological links.

faq.question: Which RAW FARM cheese products are affected by this recall?
faq.answer: The recall includes specific batches of RAW CHEDDAR Original, RAW CHEDDAR Simply Shredded, and RAW CHEDDAR Jalapeno in block and bag packaging with the batch numbers and expiration dates listed in the company announcement.

faq.question: What should I do if I have bought RAW FARM recalled cheese?
faq.answer: Do not eat the cheese from the recalled batches. Return it to the store where you bought it for a full refund or follow instructions on the product label or recall notice.

headline: Aphreseller Recalls Kian Pee Wan Dietary Supplement Capsules for Undeclared Prescription Drugs

image.caption: Aphreseller Recalls Kian Pee Wan Dietary Supplement Capsules for Undeclared Prescription Drugs

description: Aphreseller recalls Kian Pee Wan capsules nationwide after FDA found undeclared dexamethasone and cyproheptadine, posing health risks to consumers.
keywords: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for treatment for gaining weight and stimulating appetite.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Aphreseller is voluntarily recalling all lots of Kian Pee Wan capsules because FDA testing found undeclared prescription drugs dexamethasone and cyproheptadine. These ingredients make the product unsafe as a dietary supplement.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Kian Pee Wan capsules, marketed as a dietary supplement to help with weight gain and stimulate appetite, are being recalled nationwide by Aphreseller (Ebay seller ID, Buy-herbal.com). The recall was announced on March 30, 2026, after FDA laboratory analysis discovered the presence of undeclared dexamethasone, a corticosteroid, and cyproheptadine, a prescription antihistamine. These substances are not permitted in dietary supplements.

The capsules come in a white bottle with a beige label containing 30 pills. They were distributed online nationwide through eBay. Because the product contains these unapproved drug ingredients without disclosure, it is considered an unapproved drug by the FDA, and its safety and effectiveness have not been established.

Aphreseller is notifying customers via this announcement and email, arranging product returns. Consumers are advised to stop using the capsules immediately and return them to the point of purchase or dispose of them safely.

facts.product: Dietary supplement capsules marketed for treatment for gaining weight and stimulating appetite.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Kian Pee Wan capsules are sold in white bottles with beige labels and contain 30 capsules each. The product is marketed as a dietary supplement for weight gain and appetite stimulation under the brand name "Kian Pee Wan" by Aphreseller, operating through Buy-herbal.com and eBay. Consumers should compare the product bottle and label details with the photos or descriptions in the FDA recall notice or on Aphreseller's website for confirmation.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
Consumers who have Kian Pee Wan capsules should immediately stop using them. They are advised to return the product to their place of purchase for a refund, or if purchased directly from Aphreseller, follow instructions to return to the address in Flushing, NY. Contact the company via the provided phone or email for return details.

If you have used the product and experienced any health issues, contact your healthcare provider promptly. Adverse reactions or quality problems can be reported to the FDA's MedWatch program online or by mail/fax using the contact information in the FDA announcement.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid used medically to reduce inflammation but can cause serious side effects including increased infection risk, elevated blood sugar, muscle damage, psychiatric problems, and cardiovascular issues. Prolonged or high-dose use may suppress adrenal gland function, sometimes leading to life-threatening conditions. Sudden stopping after long-term use can cause withdrawal symptoms.

Cyproheptadine is a prescription antihistamine that can cause sedation, dizziness, fatigue, and other side effects. Because these drugs are undeclared in Kian Pee Wan capsules, consumers unknowingly risk adverse reactions or harmful health effects, especially if they have underlying medical conditions or are taking other medications.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased or currently possesses Kian Pee Wan capsules from Aphreseller (Buy-herbal.com) or through eBay should check their product. This includes consumers across the United States who use the capsules for weight gain or appetite stimulation.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Dietary supplements are regulated differently from drugs and must not contain undeclared prescription drugs. Undeclared ingredients like corticosteroids or antihistamines pose serious health risks because consumers are unaware of them and cannot properly manage potential side effects or drug interactions. This recall highlights the importance of careful product source verification and urgent action when safety warnings arise.

faq.answer: These drugs can cause serious health effects and require medical supervision. Their undeclared presence means consumers may unknowingly experience harmful side effects or drug interactions.

faq.answer: You can return the product to your original place of purchase for a refund. If bought from Aphreseller via eBay, contact them using the provided phone number or email for return instructions.

headline: Tops Recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Over Botulism Risk

image.caption: Tops Recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Over Botulism Risk

description: Tops Friendly Markets recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic for potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a possible life-threatening hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Tops Friendly Markets is recalling all codes of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic due to potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum caused by insufficient temperature control, posing a risk of life-threatening botulism.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Tops Friendly Markets of Williamsville, NY has issued a recall of all codes of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic products. These peeled garlic items are sold in 6 oz. plastic bags and were distributed through Tops Markets located in New York, Pennsylvania, and Vermont.

The recall was triggered after a routine store inspection found that the products were stored at temperatures inadequate to control the growth of Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause serious and potentially fatal illness. There have been no reported illnesses associated with the recalled garlic to date.

The specific products recalled are Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic (UPC 74574-10852) and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic (UPCs 71894-00000 and 68826-75340), encompassing all product code dates.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check the product packaging to identify the recalled garlic. The affected products are:

  • Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 74574-10852
  • Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 71894-00000
  • Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 68826-75340

All product code dates of these items are subject to the recall. Verify UPC codes and product weight on the package label to confirm.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should not eat the recalled peeled garlic even if it looks or smells normal. Instead, they should return the product to any Tops Market store for a full refund. If you have questions or need further information, contact Tops Consumer Affairs at 1-800-522-2522.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: Clostridium botulinum produces a toxin that can cause botulism, a rare but potentially fatal form of food poisoning. The toxin affects the nervous system, leading to symptoms like muscle weakness, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and breathing problems. Even if the garlic appears normal and does not smell spoiled, it can still harbor harmful bacteria if stored improperly.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Anyone who has purchased Christopher Ranch or Garland Peeled Garlic in 6 oz. plastic bags from Tops Markets in New York, Pennsylvania, or Vermont should check their products. This includes all code dates of these recalled items.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text: Botulism from Clostridium botulinum is a serious health threat because the bacterial toxin can cause paralysis that may result in death without timely medical treatment. Proper refrigeration is critical in preventing the growth of this bacterium in low-acid foods like peeled garlic. Consumers should always store perishable products according to label instructions and be alert to recalls involving food safety.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: Tops Consumer Affairs | 1-800-522-2522

faq.question: What contamination caused this garlic recall?

title: Recall of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Due to Possible Clostridium Botulinum Contamination

editorialBrief.title: Recall of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Due to Possible Clostridium Botulinum Contamination
editorialBrief.headline: Tops Recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic Over Botulism Risk
editorialBrief.metaDescription: Tops Friendly Markets recalls Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic for potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum, a possible life-threatening hazard.
editorialBrief.quickAnswerHtml: Tops Friendly Markets is recalling all codes of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic due to potential contamination with Clostridium botulinum caused by insufficient temperature control, posing a risk of life-threatening botulism.
editorialBrief.recallSummaryHtml:
Tops Friendly Markets of Williamsville, NY has issued a recall of all codes of Christopher Ranch and Garland Peeled Garlic products. These peeled garlic items are sold in 6 oz. plastic bags and were distributed through Tops Markets located in New York, Pennsylvania, and Vermont.

The recall was triggered after a routine store inspection found that the products were stored at temperatures inadequate to control the growth of Clostridium botulinum, a bacterium that can cause serious and potentially fatal illness. There have been no reported illnesses associated with the recalled garlic to date.

The specific products recalled are Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic (UPC 74574-10852) and Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic (UPCs 71894-00000 and 68826-75340), encompassing all product code dates.
editorialBrief.riskOverviewHtml: Clostridium botulinum produces a toxin that can cause botulism, a rare but potentially fatal form of food poisoning. The toxin affects the nervous system, leading to symptoms like muscle weakness, difficulty speaking or swallowing, and breathing problems. Even if the garlic appears normal and does not smell spoiled, it can still harbor harmful bacteria if stored improperly.
editorialBrief.whoIsAffectedHtml: Anyone who has purchased Christopher Ranch or Garland Peeled Garlic in 6 oz. plastic bags from Tops Markets in New York, Pennsylvania, or Vermont should check their products. This includes all code dates of these recalled items.
editorialBrief.howToIdentifyProductHtml:
Consumers should check the product packaging to identify the recalled garlic. The affected products are:

  • Christopher Ranch Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 74574-10852
  • Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 71894-00000
  • Garland Fresh Peeled Garlic, 6 oz., in plastic bags with UPC 68826-75340

All product code dates of these items are subject to the recall. Verify UPC codes and product weight on the package label to confirm.
editorialBrief.whatToDoHtml: Consumers should not eat the recalled peeled garlic even if it looks or smells normal. Instead, they should return the product to any Tops Market store for a full refund. If you have questions or need further information, contact Tops Consumer Affairs at 1-800-522-2522.
editorialBrief.healthAndSafetyContextHtml: Botulism from Clostridium botulinum is a serious health threat because the bacterial toxin can cause paralysis that may result in death without timely medical treatment. Proper refrigeration is critical in preventing the growth of this bacterium in low-acid foods like peeled garlic. Consumers should always store perishable products according to label instructions and be alert to recalls involving food safety.

headline: Recall Alert: Undeclared Almonds in Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread

image.caption: Recall Alert: Undeclared Almonds in Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread

description: Schreiber Foods recalls Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese due to undeclared almonds posing allergy risks. Distributed in select western U.S. states.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Schreiber Foods, Inc. is voluntarily recalling 144 cases of Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread because the product may contain undeclared almonds due to mismatched packaging. Consumers with almond allergies should be cautious and check their product.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Schreiber Foods, Inc., located in Green Bay, Wisconsin, is recalling specific shipments of Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread. The affected product is packaged as a 6-ounce plastic cup with a Honey Almond lid but an incorrectly labeled Plain cup, which does not list almonds on its label.

The recall involves a limited number of cases distributed to Einstein Bros. Bagel store locations in Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Wyoming. The issue was caused by a packaging staging error, resulting in mismatched lids and cups with conflicting allergen labeling for almonds.

The lot involved is identified by the "Best If Used By" date Jul 21, 2026 and lot code "LO" printed on the bottom of the cup. To date, no allergic reactions or illnesses have been reported related to this recall.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check for the following details to identify the recalled product:

  • Brand: Einstein Bros Bagels
  • Product: Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread in a 6-ounce plastic cup
  • Packaging: The lid is labeled "Honey Almond", but the cup is incorrectly labeled "Plain" (without almonds)
  • Lot code: "Best If Used By Jul 21, 2026 LO" printed on the bottom of the cup

Only products with this exact lot code and mismatched packaging are part of this recall.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text:
If you have purchased Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread with the July 21, 2026 LO lot code that shows a Honey Almond lid and a Plain-labeled cup, you should not consume the product.

Return the product to the place of purchase for a full refund. If you have questions, contact Einstein Bros through their official website at www.einsteinbros.com/contact/ or reach out to Schreiber Foods’ media contact, Andrew Tobisch, via email at Andrew.Tobisch@Schreiberfoods.com or phone at (920) 455-6484.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
Almonds are a tree nut allergen that can cause serious or life-threatening allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. Because the packaging mismatches the lid and cup, consumers relying on the cup label might unknowingly consume almonds, which is hazardous for those with almond allergies or severe sensitivities.

Allergic reactions can range from mild symptoms like itching or hives to severe respiratory distress or anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention. Therefore, correct allergen labeling is critical to consumer safety.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
This recall affects consumers who purchased Einstein Bros Bagels Honey Almond Cream Cheese Spread with a mismatched lid and cup. Specifically, anyone with an allergy or severe sensitivity to almonds should check if they have this product.

The affected product was sold through Einstein Bros. Bagel locations in Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Wyoming. If you have the product matching the reported lot code and packaging mismatch, you should take action as described below.

content.subtitle: Health and Safety Context
content.text:
Tree nut allergens like almonds are among the most common food allergens and can cause serious allergic reactions. Cross-contact or undeclared presence in foods poses a significant risk to allergic consumers.

Proper labeling and manufacturing controls are essential to prevent allergen exposure. Consumers with food allergies should always read product labels carefully and stay informed about recalls involving allergens.

faq.answer: The recall was caused by a packaging error where the product had a Honey Almond lid but the cup was labeled Plain, which does not list almonds. This led to undeclared almonds on the label, risking allergic reactions.

faq.question: Which stores received the recalled product?
faq.answer: The product was distributed to Einstein Bros. Bagel locations in Colorado, Utah, New Mexico, and Wyoming.

faq.question: What should I do if I have this product and an almond allergy?
faq.answer: Do not eat the product. Return it to the place of purchase for a full refund. For additional questions, contact Einstein Bros via their website or Schreiber Foods directly.

content.text:
This summary is based solely on the company safety alert posted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration on March 28, 2026. The recall announcement from Schreiber Foods, Inc. was published by the FDA as a public service to inform consumers. The FDA does not endorse the product or company.

Information can be verified through the original FDA post or the product packaging details specified in the recall notice.
